title: Menu System
description: Build a menu system for the contact manager. Create show_menu() that displays available actions, and handle_choice() that dispatches to the right function based on user input.
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 8: Menu System
# Use if/elif/else to build a command dispatcher.

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
]

def show_menu():
    """Display the menu and return the text as a string.
    Menu options:
    1. View all contacts
    2. Search contacts
    3. Add contact
    4. Remove contact
    5. Quit
    """
    # Your code here — build and return the menu string
    return ''

def handle_choice(choice, contact_list):
    """Handle a menu choice. Returns a string describing what happened.
    
    '1' -> return 'view' (would display all contacts)
    '2' -> return 'search' (would search contacts)
    '3' -> return 'add' (would add a contact)
    '4' -> return 'remove' (would remove a contact)
    '5' -> return 'quit' (exit the program)
    anything else -> return 'invalid'
    """
    # Your code here — use if/elif/else
    return 'invalid'

# Test it
print(show_menu())
print(f"Choice 1: {handle_choice('1', contacts)}")
print(f"Choice 5: {handle_choice('5', contacts)}")
print(f"Choice 9: {handle_choice('9', contacts)}")
test_code:
menu = show_menu()
assert isinstance(menu, str), 'show_menu should return a string'
assert '1' in menu, 'Menu should contain option 1'
assert '2' in menu, 'Menu should contain option 2'
assert '3' in menu, 'Menu should contain option 3'
assert '4' in menu, 'Menu should contain option 4'
assert '5' in menu, 'Menu should contain option 5'
assert handle_choice('1', contacts) == 'view', f"Choice 1 should return 'view', got '{handle_choice('1', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('2', contacts) == 'search', f"Choice 2 should return 'search', got '{handle_choice('2', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('3', contacts) == 'add', f"Choice 3 should return 'add', got '{handle_choice('3', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('4', contacts) == 'remove', f"Choice 4 should return 'remove', got '{handle_choice('4', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('5', contacts) == 'quit', f"Choice 5 should return 'quit', got '{handle_choice('5', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('9', contacts) == 'invalid', f"Invalid choice should return 'invalid', got '{handle_choice('9', contacts)}'"
assert handle_choice('', contacts) == 'invalid', 'Empty string should be invalid'
assert handle_choice('abc', contacts) == 'invalid', 'Non-numeric should be invalid'
acceptance_criteria: show_menu() returns a string containing all 5 options
handle_choice() returns correct action string for choices 1-5
hints: Build the menu string with \n between lines and return it
Use if choice == '1': ... elif choice == '2': ... etc.
The final else catches all invalid inputs
Compare choice as a string, not an integer — input() always returns strings
